description: API-surface coverage manifest. For each domain-security plugin, the relevant target SDK / runtime surface and the count of callable APIs that have at least one Interlace rule covering them. Closes the §2 'API-surface coverage' (gap) row in distribution/EVALUATION_METRICS.md. Hand-maintained — refresh by reading the target package's public exports and counting rule coverage in our plugin's manifest.
method: For each target API surface, an entry records: (a) the total count of public callable APIs (functions, classes, methods that take user input), (b) the count covered by at least one rule (i.e. the rule fires on at least one realistic misuse of that API). Coverage is binary per-API; if a rule catches misuse of any sink on `node:fs`, that's 1 covered. The score is covered/total. Targets a ≥60% floor for each domain plugin.

plugins.surface: Node.js core modules — fs, child_process, crypto, vm, dns, http(s)
uncovered_examples: node:vm.SourceTextModule — niche, not in 99% of code paths
node:dns.lookup variants — covered by adjacent rules only
node:http.Agent — TLS config patterns are covered, agent reuse less so
node:tls.createSecureContext — partially covered via require-secure-context
plugins.notes: Coverage focuses on the high-frequency-misuse exports (`fs.readFile`/`writeFile`, `child_process.exec`, `crypto.create*`, `vm.runInThisContext`). The 30% gap is mostly low-traffic APIs where misuse is exceedingly rare.

plugins.surface: node-postgres — pg.Client / pg.Pool / pg.Query callable surface
uncovered_examples: pg.types.setTypeParser — type-coercion sink, not a SQL injection surface; correctly out of scope
plugins.notes: Near-complete coverage of the query-construction surface. The single uncovered API is type-parser registration, which is not a SQL-injection sink.

plugins.surface: mongodb driver — Collection / Db / cursor query methods
uncovered_examples: Collection.bulkWrite — covered for find/update operators, not for indexes
Db.command — raw command pass-through, low-frequency
Collection.aggregate — stage pipeline checks, only the most common stages
plugins.notes: Coverage spans the high-frequency query construction surface (find, update, delete, $where). bulkWrite + aggregate need expanded coverage; both already detected.

plugins.surface: jsonwebtoken — sign / verify / decode + jose / fast-jwt analogues
plugins.notes: Full coverage of the public sign/verify/decode surface across the three dominant JWT libraries.

plugins.surface: Express — router methods, middleware patterns, common response sinks
uncovered_examples: app.engine — view-engine misconfig (rare)
res.render — template-injection sinks (partial)
app.set('trust proxy') — IP-handling correctness
express.Router merge-params — option misuse
plugins.notes: Coverage spans the high-traffic response sinks and CORS / cookie / CSRF middleware patterns. Template-engine and view-render rules are a known follow-up area.

plugins.surface: AWS Lambda — handler signatures, env-var access, callback / context patterns, AWS SDK client misuse
uncovered_examples: Lambda extensions API — niche
Lambda response streaming — newer pattern, partial coverage
context.identity (Cognito JWT) — covered indirectly via jwt plugin
plugins.notes: Coverage spans the entry-point patterns (handler shape, env-var leak, secret-in-env), IAM role patterns, and the AWS SDK v3 client construction surface. Response streaming is an emerging surface to expand.

plugins.surface: NestJS — Controllers, Providers, Guards, Decorators, Pipes
uncovered_examples: Custom Pipes — narrowly covered (validation-pipe shape only)
ExceptionFilters — minimal coverage
Interceptors — coverage of error-leak patterns only
Microservices transports — not yet covered (Kafka / NATS / Redis)
plugins.notes: 63% is at the floor. NestJS coverage is the lightest of the security plugins (6 rules). High-value expansion targets: ExceptionFilter patterns and Microservices transport-layer security.

plugins.surface: Vercel AI SDK — generateText / streamText / generateObject / tools
uncovered_examples: useChat / useCompletion hooks — client-side patterns covered shallowly
RSC streamUI — newer; partial
Multi-step tool-call orchestration — covered in pieces, not as a unified flow
plugins.notes: 75% on a moving target (the AI SDK ships quickly). 19 rules on 12 APIs reflects that some APIs have multiple distinct misuse patterns we cover separately.

plugins.surface: Browser web platform — DOM sinks, postMessage, storage, fetch, WebSocket, ServiceWorker, IndexedDB
uncovered_examples: Push API — niche
Web Share API — niche
WebRTC signaling — covered for postMessage layer, not RTCPeerConnection sinks
WebTransport — newer, not yet covered
Permissions API — partial
plugins.notes: 71% across a large surface (58 APIs). Coverage prioritizes the high-frequency XSS / CSRF / data-leak sinks. Newer APIs (WebTransport, Compute Pressure) are tracked but uncovered.

plugins.surface: Generic JS — eval / Function / regex / serialization / credentials
uncovered_examples: Worker / SharedWorker — host-side sinks, partial
plugins.notes: Near-complete coverage of the generic OWASP-Top-10 surface. 27 rules on 19 APIs reflects multiple distinct misuses per API.

summary.note: Aggregate coverage is the unweighted mean across all 10 plugins. Per-plugin coverage is the more actionable signal — a single low-coverage plugin should drive the next investment cycle.
